info.title: openconfig-system
info.description:
Model for managing system-wide services and functions on
network devices.

Portions of this code were derived from IETF RFC 7317.
Please reproduce this note if possible.

IETF code is subject to the following copyright and license:
Copyright (c) IETF Trust and the persons identified as authors of
the code.
All rights reserved.

Redistribution and use in source and binary forms, with or without
modification, is permitted pursuant to, and subject to the license
terms contained in, the Simplified BSD License set forth in
Section 4.c of the IETF Trust's Legal Provisions Relating
to IETF Documents (http://trustee.ietf.org/license-info).

depth.name: depth
depth.description: limit the depth of nodes in response

fields.name: fields
fields.description: optionally identify specific data nodes in response

filter.name: filter
filter.description: xpath expression to filter data nodes in response

insert.name: insert
insert.description: controlling the order when adding new list elements

point.name: point
point.description: used to specify the insertion point

accounting-method-id.name: accounting-method-id
accounting-method-id.description:
An ordered list of methods used for AAA accounting for this
event type.  The method is defined by the destination for
accounting data, which may be specified as the group of
all TACACS+/RADIUS servers, a defined server group, or
the local system.

args-id.name: args-id
args-id.description:
Current process command line arguments.  Arguments with
a parameter (e.g., --option 10  or -option=10) should be
represented as a single element of the list with the
argument name and parameter together.  Flag arguments, i.e.,
those without a parameter should also be in their own list
element.

authentication-method-id.name: authentication-method-id
authentication-method-id.description:
Ordered list of authentication methods for users.  This
can be either a reference to a server group, or a well-
defined designation in the AAA_METHOD_TYPE identity.  If
authentication fails with one method, the next defined
method is tried -- failure of all methods results in the
user being denied access.

authorization-method-id.name: authorization-method-id
authorization-method-id.description:
Ordered list of methods for authorizing commands.  The first
method that provides a response (positive or negative) should
be used.  The list may contain a well-defined method such
as the set of all TACACS or RADIUS servers, or the name of
a defined AAA server group.  The system must validate
that the named server group exists.

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.description: Top-level container for OCSP server configurations.

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.name: ocsp

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.name: enabled
data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.description: Specifies whether to use OCSP checking. The default is disabled.

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.name: nonce-request
data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.description: Specifies whether queries to OCSP responders should contain nonce. The default is on.

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.name: override-responder
data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.description: Force the use of default responder URI for OCSP validation. The default is off.

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.name: response-max-age
data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.description: Specifies the maximum allowable age (freshness) for OCSP responses. The default value (-1) does not enforce a maximum age.

data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.name: response-time-skew
data_openconfig-system_system_aaa_authen.description: Specifies the maximum allowable time skew, in seconds, for OCSP response validation. The default is 300 seconds.

debug-service-service.name: debug-service-service
debug-service-service.description: Reference to the debug-enable service key.

event-event-type.name: event-event-type
event-event-type.description: Reference to the event-type list key

host-entry-hostname.name: host-entry-hostname
host-entry-hostname.description: Reference to the hostname list key

ipv4-address-id.name: ipv4-address-id
ipv4-address-id.description: List of IPv4 addressses for the host entry

ipv6-address-id.name: ipv6-address-id
ipv6-address-id.description: List of IPv6 addresses for the host entry

license-license-id.name: license-license-id
license-license-id.description: Reference to license id list key

listen-addresses-id.name: listen-addresses-id
listen-addresses-id.description:
The IP addresses that the gRPC server should listen
on. This may be an IPv4 or an IPv6 address

ntp-key-key-id.name: ntp-key-key-id
ntp-key-key-id.description: Reference to auth key-id list key

process-pid.name: process-pid
process-pid.description: Reference to the process pid key

remote-server-host.name: remote-server-host
remote-server-host.description: Reference to the host list key

search-id.name: search-id
search-id.description:
An ordered list of domains to search when resolving
a host name.

server-address.name: server-address
server-address.description: References the configured address of the DNS server

server-group-name.name: server-group-name
server-group-name.description: Reference to configured name of the server group

user-username.name: user-username
user-username.description: References the configured username for the user
